forecasts.reasoning: As of May 23, 2025, there are roughly six weeks remaining until the July 1 deadline. Discord brought on Goldman Sachs and JP Morgan in late March and appointed a new CEO in late April. Historically, the timeline from hiring bankers to filing a public S-1 spans at least 4 to 6 months, pushing a likely public filing into late summer or fall. Furthermore, reports indicate that Discord is launching new ad products "this summer" specifically to "drum up revenue potential in the lead-up to an IPO." This strongly implies that the actual IPO—and the public S-1 that precedes it by roughly 3-4 weeks—are targeted for Q4 2025 or later, allowing the new ad offerings to demonstrate impact. While they could submit a confidential Draft Registration Statement (DRS) before July 1, the resolution criteria require either a public SEC Form S-1 filing or a specific announcement on Discord's official blog. Given that companies are not obligated to announce confidential submissions—and often wait until the public S-1 flip—it is extremely unlikely Discord will meet the criteria before July 1. I am estimating a 12% probability to account for any surprises, accelerated timelines, or early blog PR.

Discord is clearly on an active path to an IPO, moving from rumors to hiring JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs in March 2025, to completing a founder-to-professional CEO transition on April 23, 2025 (with Humam Sakhnini taking over to lead the company public). While an IPO in 2025 is very highly likely, the question is whether they will file an S-1 or officially announce it on their blog by July 1, 2025 (~38 days from the forecast date of May 23). A new CEO often needs 1-3 months to get up to speed, refine the roadshow pitch, and review the S-1, making a June filing highly possible but not a certainty (it could slip to August/September for a late 2025 IPO). Given the rapid sequence of events, a public S-1 or a PR announcing a confidential SEC submission in June is very plausible, but the short timeframe warrants keeping the probability slightly above a coin-flip.
